hypothesis: If pre-trained ML surrogates (EquiformerV2 and eSEN) are used to pre-converge NEB images and then SSNEB is restarted with DFT, the ML-SSNEB workflow will require fewer DFT SSNEB iterations and lower total computational time than VASP-only SSNEB while producing minimum-energy pathways and transition-state geometries/energies for CsPbI3 and TiO2 that agree with VASP-only results within the SSNEB convergence criteria (forces < 0.01 eV/Å).
